mnemonics: R.A.W. Data (Read, Analyze, Wrangle) - Remember the three core R skills for data analytics: reading data, analyzing it, and wrangling/cleaning it.
keyPoints: Focus on mastering core tidyverse packages (dplyr, ggplot2, tidyr) for data manipulation and visualization.
Utilize R Markdown for creating reproducible reports that combine code, output, and narrative.
Practice applying statistical functions and models (like lm() for linear regression) on real datasets.
commonMistakes: Trying to learn advanced topics before mastering the fundamentals of the tidyverse, leading to frustration.
Not using factors appropriately for categorical data, causing issues in modeling and visualization.
Writing non-reproducible code by using absolute paths or not documenting the analysis workflow.
